description: Attackers exploited critical, unpatched deserialization and path traversal vulnerabilities built on earlier CVE to gain unauthenticated remote code execution on on-premises SharePoint servers. Campaign named ToolShell
section: Other reservation service and related activities
impacted_entities: Microsoft SharePoint server customers
number_impacted: ~400 organizations breached, listing 300+ confirmed compromises and ~424 vulnerable Ips exposed
immediate_op_impact: Unauthorized access to SharePoint content, persistent future access, credential theft, ransomware deployment, high risk espionage and long-term comrpomise
financial_impact_type: Breach response, incident remediation, forensic investigations, legal/regulatory fines, ransom payments, reputational damage, cyber insurance claims
response_measures: Released emergency patches for SharePoint subscription, coordination with FBI/DHS/DOD

description: Recent spike in retail focused cybercrime
impacted_entities: Sainsbury and customers
number_impacted: Unable to process orders placed after attack. Thousands of pounds worth of food products wasting away,
immediate_op_impact: Service outages, ransomware deployment
financial_impact_type: Lost sales revenue, Operational disruption costs
response_measures: Ongoing client communication

description: Sophisticated supply chain ransomware attack targeting UK retail giant through social engineering of third-party IT services provider
impacted_entities: M&S, Harrod's, Co-op
immediate_op_impact: Operational disruption (supply chain, Online services down, payment processors outage)
financial_impact_type: Lost sales revenue, Operational disruption costs, Data breach remediation, ICO fines, Stock market value loss

description: Attackers gained access to customer database with personal and financial data
impacted_entities: Wonga and customers
immediate_op_impact: Data theft
financial_impact_type: Reputational damage, loss of business, legal and investigation costs
response_measures: Established webpage for affected customers, worked with authorities to investigate breach

description: The incident was attrivuted to a technical/software failure in Barclays' UK mainframe operating system caused widespread disruption across online banking, mobile apps, telephone services, and card transactions
impacted_entities: Barclays Bank UK (plc, consumer and corporate bank)
number_impacted: Millions of UK customers, 5,000 issues had been reported with Barclays, over 50% of payments failed, outage affected online access, card use, ATM withdrawals, phone support, merchant services
immediate_op_impact: Inability to login in mobile banking, telephone functions down, ATM unavailable, payment delays
financial_impact_type: Compensation to customers for failed transactions and service disruptions
response_measures: Bank proactively communicated and updated within two hours of outage onset; UK Treasury Select Committee demanded full incident details, compensation estimates, fraud-monitoring potential, and compensation plans from Barclays

description: Smiths Group detected "unauthorised access" in its systems abd said it rapidly isolated affected systems and activated business continuity plans after it detected the activity
section: Other activities auxiliary to financial services, except insurance and pension funding
impacted_entities: Smith Group
number_impacted: NA but Smiths has more than 15,000 employees
immediate_op_impact: Affected systems taken offline, disruption to IT operations and systems restoration underway
financial_impact_type: Share price dropped but no direct financial loss estimate disclosed
response_measures: Containement of affected systems and engagement of cybersecurity experts

description: A former contractor allegedly trespassed into the museum and shut down key IT systems, including the ticketing platform, causing significant operational difficulties
section: Activities of other holding companies (not including agricultural, production, construction, distribution and financial services holding companies) nec
impacted_entities: British Museum exhibitions, ticketing, visitor access
immediate_op_impact: Gallery closures, ticketing system offline, refunds issued
financial_impact_type: Gallery closures, ticketing system offline, refunds issued
response_measures: Arrest, systems restored, refunds, insider security reviews

description: Supply chain management software provider Blue Yonder was hit by the Termite ransomware gang, reportedly stealing 680?GB of data, including email lists and internal documents. Outages disrupted backend services like workforce scheduling and inventory coordination for multiple large customers
impacted_entities: Blue Yonder's SaaS platform; clients include Starbucks, Morrisons, Sainsbury's, BIC, and others
immediate_op_impact: Starbucks had to use pen-and-paper payroll systems for ~11,000 U.S. stores; Morrisons experienced warehouse fresh produce shortages; Sainsburys activated contingency systems
response_measures: External cybersecurity firms engaged; forensic investigations; restoration of hosted services; direct communication with affected customers

description: Targeted key supplier that connects ambulances to NHS and emergency responders
impacted_entities: Emergency services and those seeking emergency services
immediate_op_impact: Ambulance rerouting, service disruption
response_measures: Preparing for continued acts of aggression

event_name: Wi-Fi Network Cyber Vandalism at UK Train Stations
description: Unauthorized changes from a legitimate administrator account led to offensive messages appearing on public Wi-Fi networks at major railway stations, including Manchester Piccadilly and Birmingham New Street
impacted_entities: Train stations and commuters
number_impacted: 19 Network Rail-managed stations, thousands of commuters
immediate_op_impact: No Wi?Fi access; flyers/landing pages replaced; passenger concern; no rail disruption
response_measures: Suspension, investigation, arrest, service vendor decoupling, Wi?Fi restoration

description: Transport for London (TfL) was targeted by a cyberattack, prompting an investigation by the National Crime Agency. Immediate actions were taken to secure the systems
impacted_entities: TfL back-office and online services
immediate_op_impact: Journey history, photocard services, and live data feeds unavailable; staff access limited
response_measures: Restrictions, investigation (NCA/NCSC), notification, password reset, phased service restoration

description: Update from Crowdstrike caused a massive system crash and disrupted operations across thousands of organizations
impacted_entities: Users of cybersecurity software of crowdstrike
number_impacted: 8.5m system crashes and thousands of organizations affected
immediate_op_impact: Widespread disruption to critical services worlwide
financial_impact_type: insurance payouts, business interuption, cyber failure coverages
response_measures: Emergency IT response, software testing, deployment strategies

description: Qilin ransomware attack on pathology partnership, encrypting systems and stealing 400GB of patient data
impacted_entities: Synnovis, Guys and St thomas,  King's College hospital, NHS trusts
immediate_op_impact: 3,000+ appointments disrupted, blood test delays, data theft

description: Third party payroll system that UK MoD use was hacked and stolen personal data of reservists and veterans from all three branches
impacted_entities: Personnel, reservists and veterans of British Armed Forces
immediate_op_impact: Data theft, espionage, reputational damage
response_measures: MoD took affected network offline and providing guidance on those impacted how to monitor their accounts of suspicious activity

description: Ransomware group ALPHV/BlackCat infiltrated Change Healthcare's systems. They exfiltrated sensititve info and deployed ransomware, shutting down pharmacy systems across the US
impacted_entities: Change Healthcare internal systems; indirect impact on pharamcies, hospitals, insurers
number_impacted: Thousands of providers and pharmacies; nearly 100% of US claims processing volume temporarily affected
immediate_op_impact: Nationwide pharmacy payment outages, delayed medical billing, reimbusement, and prior authorizations
financial_impact_type: 469.6 lost revenue & system restoration; 118.8 in temp provider loans; 79.2+ in customer credits; 17.4 ransomware
response_measures: Paid ~ $22m ransom; rebuilt claims platform; issued temporary financing to providers

description: Security breach resulting in stolen personal data and financial information for sale on the dark web
section: Manufacture of non-electronic instruments and appliances for measuring, testing and navigation, except industrial process control equipment
impacted_entities: Southern Water clients
immediate_op_impact: No major disruption in UK, but data access risk
financial_impact_type: Data breach management, legal advising, potential ransom demand
response_measures: Hired independent cybersecurity experts to monitor the dark web and contacting anyone whose data has been leaked

description: The regresSSHion vulnerability is an unauthenticated remote code execution flaw found in OpenSSH servers on glibc-based Linux systems. It allowed root access to the targeted machine without user interaction.
impacted_entities: OpenSSH server deployments
number_impacted: Over 14 million public servers; 16, 400 organizations exposed
immediate_op_impact: No documented actual exploitation or compromises
response_measures: Advisories recommended immediate upgrading

description: ALPHV (BlackCat) ransomware group infiltrated MeridianLinks corporate network, exfiltrating sensitive customer and operational data; encryption not fully deployed as company shut down systems quickly.
impacted_entities: MeridianLink corporate IT; potential downstream impact on bank/credit union clients
immediate_op_impact: Minor service interuptions; potential data exposure for financial institution clients
response_measures: Forensic investigation, systems isolated quickly to prevent encryption
stock_original: Didn't really see a direct stock decrease

description: Hacker group, Rhysida exploited lack of MFA and phising to exfiltrate ~600GB of data, including HR and financial records.
impacted_entities: British library internal systems; people database for 20,000 writers/translators
immediate_op_impact: Catolgue offline, ticketing and payments disabled; fellowship programs suspended; royalties deleted
response_measures: Engaged NCSC and cybersecurity firms; rebuilt infrastructure; delayed royalty payments

description: Personal details of 147.9m US and 13.8m UK consumers were accessed
section: Activities of financial services holding companies
impacted_entities: Customers of Equifax
number_impacted: 15m UK customer records and 145m US customers
immediate_op_impact: Data theft, reputational damage
response_measures: Invested over $1.5b in security and tech transformation

description: Social engineering attack exploited MGMs help desk, enabling ALPHV/BlackCat & affiliate Scattered Spider to compromise Active Directory, deploy ransomware, and exfiltrate customer data (drivers licenses, SSNs).
impacted_entities: MGM corporate systems; indirect impact on casino guests, loyalty program members
immediate_op_impact: Shut down hotel check-in systems, slot machines, ATMs; manual room key distribution; revenue lost from casino downtime
financial_impact_type: lost in revenue; additional remediation and legal costs ongoing; IT improvements
response_measures: manual workarounds, gradual system rebuilds, no ransom paid

description: Scattered Spider exploited IT vendor social engineering to steal loyalty program database (~6.9 million members). Unlike MGM, Caesars avoided major operational disruption by paying ransom.
impacted_entities: Caesars Rewards loyalty program members
immediate_op_impact: Continued normal operations
financial_impact_type: ransom demand, potential for legal costs
response_measures: Paid ransom to prevent data leak, notified affected customers
stock_original: Didn't really see a direct stock decrease

description: Unspecified threat actors infiltrated Cloroxs IT systems, forcing the company to take certain systems offline. While Clorox never formally confirmed ransomware, multiple cybersecurity analysts attribute it to a ransomware-style attack disrupting order processing and manufacturing.
section: Polish and Other Sanitation Good Manufacturing
impacted_entities: Clorox internal IT & supply chain systems; indirect impact on distributors and retailers
immediate_op_impact: Production slowdowns, shipment delays, retail stockouts
financial_impact_type: £39.5m direct incident costs; £282m+ in lost net sales
response_measures: Systems taken offline, manual order processing, forensic investigation

description: Hackers accessed a third-party vendor (Pilot Credentials) used for pilot and cadet recruitment, exposing sensitive personally identifiable information (PII) of applicants from American Airlines and Southwest Airlines.
impacted_entities: Pilot applicants and cadet program applicants
immediate_op_impact: No direct operational impact
response_measures: Notified affected individuals, provided 2 years of free credit monitoring, terminated unauthorized access at vendor

event_name: MOVEit Transfer Exploitation  Cl0p Ransomware Campaign
description: Cl0p ransomware gang exploited a zero-day SQL injection vulnerability in MOVEit Transfer, a managed file transfer (MFT) solution, stealing data from hundreds of organizations worldwide. Attack was purely data theft; encryption not used.
impacted_entities: Federal & state U.S. agencies, Shell, UCLA, Genworth, U.S. Department of Energy contractors, etc.
immediate_op_impact: No system encyption; primary impact was data exfiltration leading to regulatory and legal issues
financial_impact_type: Legal & regulatory costs, customer notification, reputational damage
response_measures: Progess Software issues emergency patches (May 31), victims shut down MOVEit servers

description: Volt Typhoon, a Chinese state-sponsored APT, conducted long-term cyber-espionage against critical infrastructure. Used living off the land techniques (PowerShell, VPN appliances) to maintain stealthy access to networks in communications, energy, transportation, and government sectors. No ransomware or data destruction; espionage and pre-positioning for potential future disruption.
impacted_entities: U.S. government agencies, telecom providers, energy companies, maritime and transportation organizations
immediate_op_impact: No confirmed operational disruption; espionage and network recon for future sabotage
financial_impact_type: Incident response, IT remediation, increased cybersecurity spend
response_measures: Joint U.S. government & Five Eyes advisories, urgent patching, removal of Volt Typhoon tools; Microsoft provided endpoint detection guidance

description: Ransomware breach stage attack credited to Black Basta, leading to limited exfiltration of data from less than 0.1% of Capita's server estate
impacted_entities: Pension scheme members, corporate retirement schemes, staff, license payers, suppliers
number_impacted: 470,000 USS pension scheme members, 350UK corporate retirement schemes
immediate_op_impact: Internal Microsoft 365 services disrupted, some client-facing IT outages in local councils and pension platforms
response_measures: Internal and external forensic investigation, ICO notification, client advisories, legal class-action filed

description: Conti ransomware gang launched a coordinated ransomware campaign against 27 Costa Rican government institutions, crippling tax collection, customs systems, and social security payments. Attackers demanded $10 million USD ransom (later raised to $20 million). The new Costa Rican government (under President Rodrigo Chaves) declared a national emergency, the first country to do so due to a cyberattack.
impacted_entities: Ministry of Finance, Ministry of Labor, Costa Rican Social Security Fund (CCSS), and other public agencies
immediate_op_impact: Shutdown of customs and tax systems, delayed import/export trade, halted salary and pension payments for weeks; hospitals and healthcare services disrupted
financial_impact_type: Costa Rican government estimated $30 million USD/week in lost trade & tax revenue
response_measures: Declared state of emergency; refused to pay ransom; received international assitance; replaced compromised systems; migrated to cloud infrastructure

description: Attack impacted operations resulting in the temporary shutdown of production plants and the disruption of food supplies to U.S. grocery stores by targeting company servers one-quarter of end-user computers.
impacted_entities: General public, Dole business entities
immediate_op_impact: Business interruptions for Fresh Vegetables and Chilean business
response_measures: Took steps to investigate and contain the attack, retaining the services of leading third-party cybersecurity experts. Dole also has been working with law enforcement

description: North Korean state-backed threat actors (Lazarus Group) compromised 3CXs software build environment, injecting malware into digitally signed versions of the 3CXDesktopApp VoIP software. Malicious updates were pushed to thousands of customers globally, enabling information theft and potential lateral movement.
impacted_entities: 3CX customers using the compromised desktop app
immediate_op_impact: Primarily an espionage/intelligence-gathering campaign; no widespread service outages but required mass uninstalls and security sweeps
financial_impact_type: Remediation, IT security sweeps, reputational damage for 3CX
response_measures: 3CX advised uninstalling desktop app, moved to web app version; issued clean software builds; engaged Mandiant for forensic review; CrowdStrike & other vendors provided IoCs for detection

description: Ransomware group (suspected Black Basta, an ALPHV affiliate) infiltrated Dishs corporate IT network, deployed ransomware, and exfiltrated sensitive employee & customer data. Outage disrupted TV services, Boost Mobile wireless operations, and call centers.
impacted_entities: Dish corporate IT systems, call centers, Boost Mobile operations, individuals data
immediate_op_impact: Satellite TV and streaming outages, Boost Mobile service disruptions, billing delays, customer service blackout
financial_impact_type: Estimated business interupption and remediation costs
response_measures: Systems rebuilt, forensic investigation, manual billing adjustments
stock_original: Didn't really see a direct stock decrease

description: Threat actors exploited an API vulnerability to access customer account data (names, billing addresses, emails, phone numbers, dates of birth, T-Mobile account numbers, plan details). No SSNs or financial information were reportedly exposed.
impacted_entities: T Mobile postpaid & prepaid customer accounts
immediate_op_impact: No services outages; reputational damage and regulatory scrutiny
response_measures: API vulnerability patched, enhanced monitoring, customer notifications
stock_original: No material drop due to event

description: LockBit ransomware encrypted and exfiltrated IT systems used to print customs labels for international mail, also threatening to publish stolen data on its dark?web leak site
impacted_entities: Royal Mail
number_impacted: Over 500,000 international letters and parcels were stuck; impact spanned 11,500 Post Office branches across the UK for export items
immediate_op_impact: International export services were suspended, customers were instructed not to submit new international parcels. Parcels queued at processing centers; small businesses faced refunds and order cancellations
financial_impact_type: Spent on recovery and systems resilience improvements at Heathrow centre
response_measures: Engaged forensic experts, refused to pay ransom
stock_original: No material drop due to event

description: Rackspace's hosted Microsoft Exchange environment experienced a security incident attributed to a ransomware attack. Hosts were proactively powered down and disconnected to contain the incident.
section: Computing Infrastructure Providers, Data Processing, Web Hosting, and Related Services
impacted_entities: Rackspace Technology Inc
number_impacted: 30,000 hosted exchange customers, PST files accessed for 27 customers reported
immediate_op_impact: Hosted Exchange service shut down completely, customers directed to Microsoft 365
financial_impact_type: remediation, legal, support, and professional services
response_measures: Rackspace isolated and powered down Hosted Exchange cluster immediately, hired CrowdStrike and additional cyber-defense experts, offered free microsoft exchange plan1 licenses, issues frequent status updates

description: Threat actors gained access to Medibanks customer databases using stolen credentials, exfiltrating sensitive personal and health claims data. The group later demanded ransom and began leaking data, including sensitive abortion and mental health records, on dark web forums.
impacted_entities: Medibank customers and international student health plan users
immediate_op_impact: No disruption to core insurance services, but massive reputational and regulatory damage
financial_impact_type: Legal, regulatory and remediation costs; reputational impact
response_measures: Refused to pay ransom (government backed decision); engaged Australian Cyber Security Centre; provided credit monitoring and identity protection services to affected customers

description: Threat actors gained unauthorized access to a limited number of American Airlines employee email accounts, exposing sensitive employee and customer data, including names, drivers license numbers, passport numbers, and medical information. No evidence of misuse reported at the time.
impacted_entities: Current and former employees, and some customers
immediate_op_impact: No direct operational impact
response_measures: Secured compromised accounts, forced password resets, offered 2-year free credit monitoring to affected individuals

event_name: South Staffordshire Water and Cambridge Water Cyberattack
description: Cl0p ransomware group claimed to have exfiltrated 5?TB of data, including customer names, addresses, direct?debit details, and alleged screenshots of SCADA systems. Water supply was unaffected. Corporate IT systems disrupted; data was later published on the dark web.
impacted_entities: South Stafforshire PLC, South Staffs Water, Cambridge Water
immediate_op_impact: No disruption to water supply; customer-facing corporate systems and IT disrupted; reputational fallout and regulatory scrutiny
response_measures: Notified ICO; engaged with National Cyber Security Centre; secured storage; removed unsecure hosting; monitoring for malicious use; enhanced third-party governance and data storage practices

description: Omnicell's IT systems and third-party cloud services were affected by ransomware attacks leading to data security concerns for employees and patients
impacted_entities: Omnicell employees and patients
response_measures: Working with law enforcement and cybersecurity experts

description: Attack resulting in exposed personal data of over 40 million UK voters between 2014-2022
impacted_entities: UK voters between 2014-2022, Electoral Commission
immediate_op_impact: No major disruption in UK, but data access risk
response_measures: Strengthen security systems with password policy control and multi-factor authentication for all user with help of ICO and National Cyber Security Centre

description: A sudden power failure in a data center within Availability Zone?1 of the US?EAST?2 Region (Columbus, Ohio) caused EC2 instances and associated services to go down. Services hosted in AZ1 experienced service unavailability.
impacted_entities: AWS customers hosted in AZ1
number_impacted: Webex, Okta, Splunk, BambooHR, and others
immediate_op_impact: EC2 and many AWS services in AZ1 were unavailable or degraded; users of impacted SaaS (e.g. Webex, Okta, Zoom) experienced sign-in errors, service outage, inability to access applications
response_measures: AWS shifted traffic away from AZ1; restored power quickly; recommended failover to other AZs; disabled the scaling activity that triggered the event; promised internal monitoring/process improvements and future health dashboard enhancements
stock_original: No material drop due to event

description: Hackers infiltrated data system, accessed sensitive data, and deployed ransomware on Advanced Computer Software Group, which affected emergency healthcare services
impacted_entities: NHS Services and care providers
immediate_op_impact: NHS services offine; delayed ambulance dispatch, prescriptions, referrals
response_measures: ICO settlement, system rebuilds, vulnerability scanning

description: Data extortion group conducting big game hunting attacks via social engineering and insider threats
impacted_entities: Tech firms, UK-based orgs
immediate_op_impact: Data theft, extortion, service disruption
response_measures: Not publicly disclosed

description: Ransomware attack affecting Safestyle UK's IT systems; data security concerns raised though no mass leak confirmed
impacted_entities: Safestyle UK internal systems (order processing and potentially customer data
immediate_op_impact: Temporary disrupition to sales, scheduling, and customer service systems
response_measures: Isolation of affected systems, investigation by third-party specialists

description: Unsecured Microsoft Azure server exposed personal data/logins for thousands of students
impacted_entities: Student logins
number_impacted: ~10,000 records accessible; potentially hundreds of thousands impacted
immediate_op_impact: No system downtime; privacy risk and phishing potential
response_measures: Secured data, reported to ICO, notified users, reviewed vendor oversight

description: Crtical remote-code-execution vulnerability in widely-used Java logging library. Attackers submit malicious payloads
impacted_entities: Millions of applications using Log4j in enterprise and cloud including AWS, Microsoft, Cloudfare, Tesla, Paypal
number_impacted: 93% of enterprise cloud environments were exposed nad hundreds of millions of servers/devices globally
immediate_op_impact: Widespread system compromise, installation of malware, crypto miners, ransomware, supply-chain exposure, disruption in infrastructure
financial_impact_type: Costs from incident response, detection, patching, forensic investigations, potential regulatory fines, ransomware payouts

description: Supply chain ransomware attack exploiting VSA software vulnerabilities to deploy REvil ransomware
impacted_entities: IT service providers, UK businesses
immediate_op_impact: Service outages, ransomware deployment

description: REvil (Sodinokibi) ransomware group infiltrated JBS USAs North American and Australian IT systems, encrypting servers and forcing multiple meat processing plant closures.
impacted_entities: JBS USA IT systems, affecting meat processing plants; downstream impact on suppliers and grocers
immediate_op_impact: Temporary closure of major US beef plants; disruption to ~20% of US beef production capacity; livestock supply chain backlog
financial_impact_type: Ransom paid £8.9m; £24 - 32m in business interuption costs
response_measures: Paid ransom to prevent data theft, restored systems via backups, enhanced cybersecurity post-event

description: DarkSide ransomware group infiltrated Colonial Pipelines billing and IT systems, forcing a proactive shutdown of ~5,500 miles of fuel pipeline supplying ~45% of East Coast fuel. Triggered panic buying, fuel shortages, and temporary gas price spikes.
impacted_entities: Colonial Pipeline internal IT systems; downstream customers indirectly impacted
immediate_op_impact: Full pipeline shutdown, major fuel shortages on the East Coast, panic buying, emergency federal waivers issued
financial_impact_type: Ransom paid £3.6m; £8 - 12 in recovery and business interuption
response_measures: Paid ransom for decryption key, manually restored systems, implemented stronger network segmentation

description: Personal details were scrapped and aggregated into a databse later leaked publicly
section: Media Streaming Distribution Services, Social Networks, and Other Media Networks and Content Providers
impacted_entities: Meta Platforms and global user base
immediate_op_impact: No service disruption reported on Facebook platforms, users were not notified individually, potential phishing and identity theft risk increased
financial_impact_type: Fines imposed by Ireland's Data Protection Commission
response_measures: Facebook confirmed the underlying vulnerability in 2019, Irish Data Protection Commission launched an enforcement action in April 2021 and fined Meta millions, mass-action lawsuits filed in Europe

description: Hackers accessed some Npower customers' mobile app accounts via credential-stuffing
impacted_entities: Undisclosed number of customer accounts; all affected were contacted and locked
immediate_op_impact: Npower immediately locked affected accounts, blocked suspicious Ips, and permanently withdrew its mobile app

description: Hackers gained unauthorized access to a system storing personal data of current and former customers, stealing full customer database including personal identifiers
impacted_entities: Current and former customers
immediate_op_impact: No disruption to energy supply or business operations reported. Impact limited to customer contact, investigation overhead, and reputational harm
response_measures: Immediate system lockdown and security fix, notified ICO, Ofgem, NCSC and police; hired external security experts

description: Nation-state espionage via trojanized Orion software updates, creating backdoors in customer networks
section: Retail sale in non-specialised stores with food, beverages or tobacco predominating
impacted_entities: UK gov depts, NHS, private sector
number_impacted: 18,000 companies installed compromised updates, but <100 confirmed compromises
immediate_op_impact: No major disruption in UK, but data access risk
response_measures: Not disclosed

description: Attackers accessed Blackbaud backup servers, exfiltrated personal and financial data for millions, then demanded ransom. Blackbaud paid 24?BTC ($235k250k USD) in ransom to delete copies.
section: Computing Infrastructure Providers, Data Processing, Web Hosting, and Related Services
impacted_entities: Blackbaud inc; and 13,000 nonprofit clients
number_impacted: 13,000 client orgs; personal records from millions of individuals
immediate_op_impact: No major system outages; reputational damage
response_measures: Paid ransom, engaged forensic responders, notified clients, expanded security program, settled with FTC/SEC and state AGs, implemented corporate governance reforms (new CISO, data retention policies)

description: Hackers hijacked 130 high-profile twitter accounts (e.g., Obama, Musk) to promote a crypto scam, exposing platform vulnerabilities.
section: Web Search Portals and All Other Information Services
impacted_entities: Twitter, high-profile account owners
number_impacted: Approx. 130 verified accounts targeted, posted scam tweets, combined millions of followers exposed
threat_actors: Graham Clark, Mason Sheppard, Nima Fazeli
immediate_op_impact: Verified accounts disabled from tweets, twitter locked down internal tool access, public trust shaken
response_measures: Twitter suspended tweeting from all verified users, access to internal admin tools restricted, US DOJ charged individuals, heightened security to twitter

description: Large scale cyber espionage campaign involving spear-phishing, credential harvesting, web-shell deployment, and persistent access into govt infrastructure
impacted_entities: Federal government agencies, utilities, law firms, universities, industry bodies
immediate_op_impact: No public service disruption reported, sensitive data access and sustained reconnaissance left networks exposed, heightened alert status declared
response_measures: Announced major cyber investment

description: Phishing email was sent by a malicious attacker. The attacker gained control of the personal info of 113,000 current/former employees
section: Other activities auxiliary to financial services, except insurance and pension funding
impacted_entities: Current and former employees
immediate_op_impact: Compromised employee accounts and systems and encypted personal data
response_measures: Launched a crisis response, appointed new CIO

description: Data breach allowed access to 9m customer records and affected customers booked flights
impacted_entities: Customers of EasyJet
number_impacted: 9m customer records and credit card details of 2208 customers
immediate_op_impact: Data theft, reputational damage, lawsuit
response_measures: Launched forensic investigation
stock_recovery_time: Still hasn't recovered (5+ years); Stock effect confounded by COVID

description: Maze ransomware group infiltrated Cognizants internal IT systems, exfiltrated sensitive client and internal data, encrypted servers, and disrupted managed services delivery. Double extortion tactics were employed.
impacted_entities: Cognizant tech solutions
immediate_op_impact: Managed service disruption, client access disabled, delays in WFH solutions rollout
response_measures: Cognizant mobilized leadership, shut down impacted systems, engaged external forensic teams, communicated with clients and regulators, and accelerated security hardening

description: Attackers used login credentials of two Marriott franchise employees to access guest information from an application used for hotel operations. The breach was distinct from the 2018 Starwood/Marriott mega-breach.
impacted_entities: Marriot guest database; mostly loyalty members
immediate_op_impact: No direct operational impact
response_measures: Disabled compromised accounts, forced password resets, notified affected customers
stock_original: NA because of noise from COVID

description: A Virgin Media employee incorrectly configured the database, leaving it publicly accessable without a password. This was not as much of an attack as a just data exposure.
impacted_entities: Virgin Media and its current and potential customers
immediate_op_impact: Unknown amount of data stolen or if stolen at all
financial_impact_type: speculated claims from legal firms from class action lawsuit
response_measures: Immediately secured the database, notified customers, published a statement and Q&A page for customers

description: Hackers held Travelex to ransome of 4.6m and forced firm to turn off computer systems and resort to using pen and paper by exploiting vulnerability in VPN. Posess sensitive customer information.
impacted_entities: Travelex
immediate_op_impact: Data theft, extortion, 4 months of business disruption
response_measures: Took down website across 30 countries to protect the data, working with police and IT specialists. 4 months of business interruption and lost 25m in following quarter in Q1 of 2020 due to cyberattack. Went into administration and company restructuring to reduce debt.

description: An individual gained access to the sensitive files by exploiting a misconfiguration in one of Capital Ones application firewalls and obtained certain types of personal information about Capital One credit card customers and individuals who had applied for credit card products.
impacted_entities: Capital one customers and staff
number_impacted: Affected approximately 100 million individuals in the United States and  6 million in Canada
immediate_op_impact: Notified by mail the U.S. individuals whose Social Security numbers or linked bank account numbers were accessed
financial_impact_type: Customer notifications, credit monitoring services, technology upgrades, fines, legal support
response_measures: Customer notification and updating security protocals. Class action lawsuit

description: Magecart attack injecting malicious JavaScript to steal payment card data from website and mobile app
section: Retail sale of clothing in specialised stores
impacted_entities: British Airways, customers
immediate_op_impact: Customer data theft, reputational damage
response_measures: https://www.bankinfosecurity.com/hacker-flies-away-british-airways-customer-data-a-11474

description: Malicious third-party JavaScript code injected via Inbenta chatbot on payment pages exfiltrated customer login, contact, and payment card details from Ticketmaster Intl, UK
impacted_entities: Customers across Europe
number_impacted: 9.4 million european customers, 1.5 million UK customers, approx. 60,000 Barclays cards and 6,000 Monzo cards subject to fraud
immediate_op_impact: No platform-wide outage; ticketmaster disabled the Inbenta chatbot and issues alerts
financial_impact_type: Costs for forensic teams and fines
response_measures: Ticketmaster notified the ICO, banks, and customers within regulatiry timeframes offered free identity monitoring and password resets

description: Attacker installed malicious software on 5,390 tills in branches of its Currys OC World and Dixons Travel chains leaving customers vulnerable to financial theft and identity fraud
impacted_entities: Customers of Dixons Carphone
number_impacted: Over 14m personal records and 5.6m financial information
immediate_op_impact: Software encription, Data theft, reputational damage
financial_impact_type: fines, declining profits, customer trust. Led to closure and rebranding to Currys
response_measures: Investment in information security systems. Launched investigation, added extra security measures and contained the incident

description: A massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attack exploiting misconfigured memcached servers as reflectors, amplifying small spoofed packets into traffic volumes up to 51,000x larger
impacted_entities: GitHub's global user base, though no data compromise occurred. Service unavailability several minutes globally
number_impacted: approx. 20 million developers, millions of hosted repositories
immediate_op_impact: GitHub was unreachable globally with intermittent availability. Downtime affected developpers committing code
response_measures: GitHub automatically rerouted traffic to Akamai Prolexic within minutes via BGP changes. Withdrawal of normal transit; traffic routed via scrub centers

description: NotPetya, a wiper malware disguised as ransomware (attributed to Russian state actors), spread via infected tax software (M.E.Doc), encrypting and destroying data across Mercks global network.
impacted_entities: Merck internal IT & manufacturing plants; vaccine production and drug formulation facilities
immediate_op_impact: Manufacturing plants halted, delays in vaccine production, temporary supply shortages
financial_impact_type: Business interuption, remediation costs; $1.4 billion USD insurance claim after court ruling (2022)
response_measures: Rebuilt networks, enhanced segmentation, pursued extensive insurance litigation

description: Brute-force attack targeted up to 9,000 email accounts belonging to MPs, Lords, staff, and contractors. Weak password usage allowed compromise of fewer than 1% of accounts
impacted_entities: Parliamentary email accounts
number_impacted: 0.5% of 9,000 parliamentary email accounts. 26 users and 39 mailboxes compromised
immediate_op_impact: Remote email access was disabled for MPs and staff; mobile/tablet access restricted. Core parliamentary services remained operational
response_measures: NCSC and National Crime Agency engaged immediately; rapid rollout of mlti-factor authentication; affected individuals notified

description: Ransomware worm exploiting Windows SMB vulnerability (EternalBlue) to encrypt files and demand Bitcoin ransom
impacted_entities: NHS Trusts, GP Practices, Hospitals
immediate_op_impact: 19,000+ appointments cancelled, ambulance rerouting, system shutdowns
response_measures: Not publicly disclosed

description: Cybercriminals conducted credential-stuffing attacks using reused emails/passwords obtained from third-party breaches to access ~26,500 Camelot online lottery accounts. Fewer than 50 accounts showed actual usage. No core systems were compromised and no funds removed
impacted_entities: Camelot Group and its online players
number_impacted: 26,500 online players, fewer than 50 had personal details changed
immediate_op_impact: Suspension of players
response_measures: Suspension of affected accounts, improved security measures

description: Attackers gained entry to the system and order phone upgrades to customers, which they planned to intercept before reaching the customer
section: Libraries, archives, museums and other cultural activities
impacted_entities: Three UK and customers
immediate_op_impact: Customer data theft, 400 stolen handsets
response_measures: Stengthened data controls, contacted customers

description: Hackers generated authentic debit card numbers and made transactions that took money from customer accounts
section: Urban, suburban or metropolitan area passenger railway transportation by underground, metro and similar systems
impacted_entities: Tesco Bank, Customers of Tesco Bank
immediate_op_impact: Financial theft, business disruption, reputational damage
response_measures: Freezing online systems. Changed Tesco Bank's debit card distribution methods from sequential order

description: Customer details stolen from company's old website
impacted_entities: JD Witherspoon clients and staff
immediate_op_impact: Data theft, reputational damage
response_measures: Increased investment in security

description: Occurred when TalkTalk acquired Tiscalis's UK operations, gave hackers opportunity to access data of personal and financial details of customers using SQL injection. Subjected the CEO to blackmail attempts
event_type: SQL injection data breach; follow-on extortion
impacted_entities: Customers of TalkTalk
number_impacted: Personal Data: 157,000; financial data: 15,656 full bank data
threat_actors: Matthew Hanley, Connor Allsopp, other unknown attackers
immediate_op_impact: Data theft, extortion, reputational damage
financial_impact_type: ICO fines, data breach management, trading impact
response_measures: Received fines fr breach of data protection obligations by ICO
stock_recovery_time: Still hasn't recovered to Oct 20, 2015 close(9+ years)

description: Sophisticated attackers infiltrated OPMs systems, exfiltrating sensitive personal data of federal employees, including SF-86 security clearance forms, fingerprints, SSNs, addresses, medical history, and more. Breach included highly confidential information about current and former U.S. government personnel.
impacted_entities: Office of Personnel Management, Department of Defense (through clearance systems), contractors
number_impacted: ~21.5 million individuals impacted, including 4.2 million federal employees and 17.3 million additional individuals tied to background checks
threat_actors: Chinese state-sponsored APT (APT10 or Deep Panda)
immediate_op_impact: National security concerns; loss of confidentiality in personnel vetting; U.S. intelligence officials had to revise clearance protocols
financial_impact_type: Identity theft prevention, notification, system rebuild, long-term counterintelligence risk
response_measures: Leadership resignations (OPM Director Katherine Archuleta); multi-year IT overhaul; DHS & FBI involvement; government-funded identity monitoring services

description: Using a destructive malware called "Wiper", hackers erased data from the company's servers and deployed ransomware. The breach exposed confidential internal emails, unreleased movies, and sensitive employee data
impacted_entities: Sony pictures staff and clients. Shareholders and reputation
financial_impact_type: Fines, lawsuits, investigations, system repair, PR efforts
response_measures: Reevaluated cybersecurity protocols

description: Remote code readers could extract memory contents (including passwords, session tokens, private keys) from affected OpenSSL versions; affected ~two-thirds of TLS?enabled servers globally.
impacted_entities: Hundreds of thousands of organizations, certificate authorities, websites, TLS clients and servers
immediate_op_impact: No downtime; widespread credential compromise
response_measures: Urgent patch deployment (OpenSSL 1.0.1g+), widespread key revocations & re-issuing, password resets, certificate authority overload mitigation, CISA and UK NCSC advisories

description: Hackers used credentials from an HVAC vendor (Fazio) to deploy POS malware (BlackPOS), stealing ~40 million credit/debit cards and PII on up to ~70 million additional customers.
impacted_entities: Target stores in U.S. and Canadian regions; corporate systems compromised
immediate_op_impact: Consumer trust damage, decline in sales
financial_impact_type: Lost revenue; breach expenses; settlements with states and banks
response_measures: CEO and CIO resigned; network segmentation overhaul; PCI compliance accelerated; new CISO appointed; deployment of EMV chip cards across retail footprint

description: Trojan ransomware encrypting files using RSA public-key cryptography, demanding Bitcoin payment
impacted_entities: General public, businesses
number_impacted:
250,000+ systems, Up to 500,000 global victims; no authoritative UK
count
immediate_op_impact: File encryption, business disruption
response_measures: Not publicly disclosed

description: A highly sophisticated state-designed cyber-weapon targeting Siemens SCADA systems controlling centrifuges at Irans Natanz nuclear facility. The worm caused physical destruction by altering motor frequencies, destroying ~9001,000 centrifuges.
impacted_entities: Natanz Uranium Enrichment Facility (Iranian nuclear program); collateral infections among global SCADA environments
immediate_op_impact: Destruction of centrifuges, delay in Irans nuclear program; risk of global SCADA penetration across industrial networks
response_measures: Siemens & national CERTs issued detection & removal tools; upgraded patching procedures; bans on USB usage; international cyber defense advisories

description: A tiny (376?byte) buffer-overflow worm targeting unpatched Microsoft SQL Server and MSDE platforms. It infected ~75,000 servers within 10 minutes and caused massive global routing failures across Internet core infrastructure.
impacted_entities: Enterprise and public Internet infrastructuremajor ISPs, root DNS servers, corporate networks
immediate_op_impact: Routing systems collapsed, web/email outages (e.g. corporate portals, ATMs, airline ticketing); estimated loss of connectivity for many organizations
response_measures: Urgent patch deployment (MS02?039), ISP traffic filtering of UDP 1434 port, emergency incident response, network segmentation, routing adjustments

description: A buffer-overflow worm exploiting Microsoft IIS servers. Code Red I defaced websites and launched denial-of-service (DoS); Code Red II added a backdoor and spread more aggressively
impacted_entities: Primarily servers; public infrastructure including White House, DoD, etc.
immediate_op_impact: Web outages; government and corporate websites shut; White House changed IP; IRS, FedEx, military sites impacted
response_measures: Emergency Microsoft patch deployment (MS01-033), network isolations, antivirus updates, national alerts via CERT, FBI, Microsoft

description: A VBScript worm disguised as a love?letter email infecting Outlook users. Once clicked, it overwrote image and document files and sent itself to all contacts, propagating globally in hours.
impacted_entities: Multiple  affected Pentagon, NASA, British Parliament, government departments, corporate inboxes
immediate_op_impact: Global email outages; critical networks shutdown
response_measures: Shutdown of email servers; emergency virus removal; patching email systems; user training; vendor cooperation; national and corporate anti-virus updates
